rulings.Case Title: ABDALLAH ELHANIA ET AL VS. AIRBNB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Sanctions Against Defendants Airbnb, Inc. And Airbnb Payments, Inc. And Their Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 4, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS AIRBNB, INC. AND AIRBNB PAYMENTS, INC. AND THEIR ATTORNEY is DENIED. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES MANN ET AL VS. SIDE, INC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 4, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. As it appears from uncontroverted declaration of Defendant's counsel, Plaintiff failed to respond to Defendant's meet and confer efforts in good faith. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in-person or via telephone the week of November 11, 2024 on a mutually agreeable date and time. If parties cannot agree on a date, the parties must meet and confer on November 13, 2024 at 2:00 p.m. No later than November 18, 2024 parties to file declarations (or a joint declaration) attesting to good faith meet and confer conference in-person or over the phone. If necessary, Defendant may file a Motion to Strike no later than November 25, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MAC MANAGEMENT LLC VS. ANATOLI TAUSTYKA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 4, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED as to the causes of action for fraud and related cause of action for conspiracy with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege fraud against each defendant with requisite specificity as to each element, including damages. Demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a controversy for which no remedy at law is available and which may be addressed by a declaratory relief cause of action. Cases cited by Plaintiff in opposition for the proposition that declaratory relief is a proper cause of action do not support Plaintiff's argument. In Orozco v. WPV San Jose, LLC (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 375 "As to the cause of action for declaratory relief, the trial court ruled it was "impossible and unnecessary" to issue the requested relief and declined to do so" and this ruling was not the subject of appeal. Pavlovich v. Neidhardt (1954) 128 Cal.App.2d 559 did not involve a cause of action for declaratory relief. To the extent Plaintiff appears to argue entitled to an election to rescind the contract and seek restoration to their original position, Plaintiff shall have leave to amend to allege an appropriate cause of action in lieu of the declaratory relief. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
